title: chore(notification-center): refactor notification center context with react-query
body:
### What change does this PR introduce?

**In the beginning, I would like to apologize for this huge PR.**

There was an idea to use the `headless` package that was supposed to encapsulate the socket management and notifications business logic in one service that was using the `@tanstack/query-core` underneath. 
But I've failed to use it in the `notification-center`. I've noticed that the code was looking similar to what the `@tanstack/react-query` does, so I've decided to refactor it to use `@tanstack/react-query`.

This PR simplifies the logic across the `notification-center` and mainly in the `NovuProvider` and `NotificationsProvider`.
I'll walk you through the PR in the comments.

### Why was this change needed?

To simplify logic in the `notification-center` package.

### Other information (Screenshots)

title: fix(package): fix tests and the implementation of the get topic
body:
### What change does this PR introduce?

<!-- Explain here the changes your PR introduces and text to help us understand the context of this change. -->
This PR introduces a breaking change in the Novu package. For the `novu.layouts.get('topic-key')` endpoint we change the response object.
Also fixes some tests and mocks that were done wrongly.

### Why was this change needed?

<!-- If your PR fixes an open issue, use `Closes #999` to link your PR with the issue. #999 stands for the issue number you are fixing, Example: Closes #31 -->
Currently the implementation of retrieving a topic by its key was calling behind the scenes the filter topics endpoint with a query param for the topic key. So the response was a paginated response. But our intention was that for a certain topic key to retrieve just the Topic DTO. Unfortunately due some changes we (I) missed that. This tries to fix it to make more sense of the API, but it is a breaking change. Due the short time we have exposed it, I consider we can do it in the new version even at the cost of annoying the users.

### Other information (Screenshots)

body:
[![Mend Renovate](https://app.renovatebot.com/images/banner.svg)](https://renovatebot.com)

This PR contains the following updates:

| Package | Change | Age | Adoption | Passing | Confidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| [http-server](https://togithub.com/http-party/http-server) | [`^0.12.3` -> `^0.13.0`](https://renovatebot.com/diffs/npm/http-server/0.12.3/0.13.0) | [![age](https://badges.renovateapi.com/packages/npm/http-server/0.13.0/age-slim)](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) | [![adoption](https://badges.renovateapi.com/packages/npm/http-server/0.13.0/adoption-slim)](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) | [![passing](https://badges.renovateapi.com/packages/npm/http-server/0.13.0/compatibility-slim/0.12.3)](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) | [![confidence](https://badges.renovateapi.com/packages/npm/http-server/0.13.0/confidence-slim/0.12.3)](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) |

---

### Release Notes

<details>
<summary>http-party/http-server</summary>

### [`v0.13.0`](https://togithub.com/http-party/http-server/releases/tag/v0.13.0)

[Compare Source](https://togithub.com/http-party/http-server/compare/v0.12.3...v0.13.0)

A long time coming, the next major release for http-server! This will be the final release before a switch to actual semantic versioning. This release's major achievement is the internalization of the functionality of the now-abandoned `ecstatic` library, thus removing it as a dependency. Huge thanks to [@​zbynek](https://togithub.com/zbynek) for help on that front, as well as several other included changes.

##### Breaking changes:

-   No longer sends the header `server: http-server-${version}` with every response

##### New features:

-   All responses include `Accept-Ranges: bytes` to advertise support for partial requests

##### Fixes

-   Removes dependency on the abandoned `ecstatic` library
-   Dependency upgrades to fix several security alerts
-   `http-server -a 0.0.0.0` will now do what you told it to do, rather than overriding the address to 127.0.0.1
-   Will no longer serve binary files with a charset in the Content-Type, fixing serving WebAssembly files, among other issues
-   Support `.mjs` MimeType correctly

##### Internal

-   Switched from Travis to GH Actions for CI

</details>

---

After multiple updates in dev, there should be one row per layout in the changes table. Before, it shows all updates separately and they needed to be promoted individually (could cause unexpected behavior in the production if one change depends on another)

Now, all changes made to a layout will be aggregated into a single promotable change.

title: Install the CodeSee workflow.
body:
This pull request adds the CodeSee workflow to your repository.
This workflow runs a code analysis on each time:

  - a new PR is opened
  - a new commit is pushed to an existing PR
  - a PR is merged or a new commit is pushed directly to the default branch

The analysis enables [code visibility](https://www.codesee.io/what-is-code-visibility) for your codebase: generating maps of your codebase, keeping existing maps up-to-date, and automating code workflows.

Access the CodeSee web app at https://app.codesee.io/ or learn more at https://docs.codesee.io. If you have any questions, please reach out to support@codesee.io and we'll be happy to help.

#### 4.0.3 (2021-02-22)

v4.0.3 is a maintenance release that includes minor bug fixes and dependency updates.

##### :bug: Bug Fix

    -   [#​10590](https://togithub.com/facebook/create-react-app/pull/10590) Upgrade eslint-webpack-plugin to fix opt-out flag ([@​mrmckeb](https://togithub.com/mrmckeb))

-   `create-react-app`
    -   [#​10384](https://togithub.com/facebook/create-react-app/pull/10384) tests: update test case to match the description ([@​jamesgeorge007](https://togithub.com/jamesgeorge007))

##### Committers: 4

-   Brody McKee ([@​mrmckeb](https://togithub.com/mrmckeb))
-   Dion Woolley ([@​Awarua-](https://togithub.com/Awarua-))
-   James George ([@​jamesgeorge007](https://togithub.com/jamesgeorge007))
-   Walker Clem ([@​wclem4](https://togithub.com/wclem4))

### Migrating from 4.0.2 to 4.0.3

Inside any created project that has not been ejected, run:

```bash
npm install --save --save-exact react-scripts@4.0.3
```

or

    yarn add --exact react-scripts@4.0.3

[Compare Source](https://togithub.com/storybookjs/testing-library/compare/v0.0.10...v0.0.11)

##### Release Notes

##### Revert "feat: upgrade testing library and user event" ([#​19](https://togithub.com/storybookjs/testing-library/pull/19))

Unfortunately `@storybook/testing-library` had to downgrade `@testing-library/user-event` to version 13. User event v14 is working ok for most projects, but projects using Svelte, Vue, HTML and Web components break because of an issue in one of user-event's dependencies.

If you are not affected by this problem and want to use `@storybook/testing-library` with user-event v14, you can use the `@storybook/testing-library@beta` version.

***

##### 🐛 Bug Fix

-   Revert "feat: upgrade testing library and user event" [#​19](https://togithub.com/storybookjs/testing-library/pull/19) ([@​yannbf](https://togithub.com/yannbf))

##### Authors: 1

-   Yann Braga ([@​yannbf](https://togithub.com/yannbf))

***

### [`v0.0.10`](https://togithub.com/storybookjs/testing-library/blob/HEAD/CHANGELOG.md#v0010-Tue-Apr-19-2022)

[Compare Source](https://togithub.com/storybookjs/testing-library/compare/v0.0.9...v0.0.10)

##### Release Notes

##### feat: upgrade testing library and user event ([#​18](https://togithub.com/storybookjs/testing-library/pull/18))

`@storybook/testing-library` now uses `@testing-library/user-event` **version 14**! 🎉

There are several new features and bugfixes and you can check them in the [user-event release page](https://togithub.com/testing-library/user-event/releases/tag/v14.0.0).

***

##### 🐛 Bug Fix

-   feat: upgrade testing library and user event [#​18](https://togithub.com/storybookjs/testing-library/pull/18) ([@​yannbf](https://togithub.com/yannbf))

##### Authors: 1

-   Yann Braga ([@​yannbf](https://togithub.com/yannbf))

***

</details>

title: feat: improve execution details for step filters
body:
### What change does this PR introduce?

Improve execution details for the step filters.

I'm not sure how it would be better to display the condition for the `isOnlineInLast`, for the condition check we do use two fields on the subscriber entity `isOnline, lastSeenAt`. When the subscriber `isOnline: true` the condition `actual: 'true'`, but when `isOnline: false` we will calculate based on `lastSeenAt`, and then the `actual: '10'` will be a time difference.
So generally speaking I don't like that the `actual` might be `true` or `10`.

### Why was this change needed?

This is a part of the "Is Online Filters" project.

### Other information (Screenshots)
![Screenshot 2023-01-16 at 17 49 25](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2607232/212731524-6d2bd3d7-e2bf-4124-a772-bc5ee0d44f59.png)

title: fix: Add support for Minio local S3 storage by upgrading AWS SDK
body:
### What change does this PR introduce?

This PR updates the packages `@aws-sdk/client-s3` and `@aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner` from `^3.14.0` to `^3.234.0`. This is to fix bugs with URL presigning that prevented a localhost instance of Minio from being able to verify request signatures. See <https://github.com/aws/aws-sdk-js-v3/pull/3897>.

### Why was this change needed?

Closes #2510

title: fix: use class validators 0.14.0
body:
We are not affected by the vulnerability because of our use of class-transformers:

This CVE works because it pollutes the prototype of thee object being validated. However class-transformer doesn't read the __proto__ property and thus doesn't make assumptions of the class type. Because of this, when the ValidationPipe runs, nd because we run plainToInstance first, this __proto__ property gets replaced with the proper prototype value and class-validator will never see this __proto__: {} which would end up causing this issue.

For the time being, please be patient, it takes time for us to figure out if we have to fully release a major upgrade or if a minor bump will be fine, and if the former how we should go about it.

Closes: NV-1485

But, upgraded the version nonetheless.
author: scopsy

title: refactor: add new parsing queue for events
body:
## What?
Introduced a new parsing queue for events to remove load on spikes of traffic during the trigger event.

## Why?
Previously before the job was submitted to the queue we've done pre processing to determine and fanout for subscriber planning of jobs. Now this happened as part of the queue worker that parses the request.

title: Updating json web token library
body:
### What change does this PR introduce?

For versions <=8.5.1 of jsonwebtoken library, if a malicious actor has the ability to modify the key retrieval parameter (referring to the secretOrPublicKey argument from the [readme link](https://github.com/auth0/node-jsonwebtoken#jwtverifytoken-secretorpublickey-options-callback)) of the jwt.verify() function, they can gain remote code execution (RCE).
So this change introduces an upgrade to 9.0.0 to fix this security issue 

### Why was this change needed?

Closes #2354

title: strict null checks refactor
body:
## What?
This pull request introduces the "strictNullCheck" of typescript. This feature adds additional type-checking to the compiler and helps to prevent null and undefined values from being assigned to variables.

## Why?
- To help us better throw API Errors when specific entities not found, as right now we have weire states when a not existing subscriberId was not found for example.
- Explicitly defining null and undefined of interface members to help prevent mistakes from happening.

This PR mainly handles the needed refactoring for build to pass, further improved could be made and in some cases hacks been used to pass the build (for example usage of `as`).
